public_reputation: Oath-bearer judge who escorted fever-dead through panic without ever breaking rite or mercy.
appearance: Bright-plumed road gear, polished fittings, and visible attention to presentation mark the role before the voice does.
manner: Projects duty first, placing sworn role ahead of comfort, convenience, or easy compromise. In Panim, that reads as someone grave, measured, and ritual-aware, with constant awareness of consequence, grief, and divine mediation, and the habits of a paladins still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of paladins in and around Panim Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Panim Haven workable through the obligations of paladins while managing the immediate pressure of divine balance obligations in Panim.
hidden_pressure: Must keep oath, threshold, and public trust aligned even when expediency would be easier and more rewarding. Around Panim Haven, that usually means carrying divine balance obligations, sacrificial and mediation decorum, institutional scrutiny without letting the role of paladins visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Seraphine of the Quiet Pyre can offer access or protection if the party preserves a rite, vow, or defended passage under pressure.
Seraphine of the Quiet Pyre needs help holding a threshold, escorting the vulnerable, or investigating a breach of sworn protection near Panim Haven.
Working with Seraphine of the Quiet Pyre can force the party to choose between expediency and the stricter obligations that govern Panim Haven.
Seraphine of the Quiet Pyre needs help with a problem in Panim Haven that larger systems have pushed down onto ordinary hands.

support_scope_notes: Integrated from the prior_release canon-ready named-NPC ledger append. Unsupported fields remain restrained. Appearance extended under V30_3 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Oath-signs, defensive bearing, and ritualized readiness make Seraphine of the Quiet Pyre read as a sworn protector rather than a mere combatant. In Panim Haven, this lands inside a public field already shaped by House Panim.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Panim Haven: gates, sacred thresholds, escorts, or defended chambers

first_impression: Seraphine of the Quiet Pyre presents as Projects duty first, placing sworn role ahead of comfort, convenience, or easy compromise. In Panim, that reads as someone grave, measured, and ritual-aware, with constant awareness of consequence, grief, and divine mediation, and the habits of a paladins still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Paladins, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Principality of Panim.
what_they_refuse: Seraphine of the Quiet Pyre refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Paladins too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Seraphine of the Quiet Pyre can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Seraphine of the Quiet Pyre adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Seraphine of the Quiet Pyre is Oath-bearer judge who escorted fever-dead through panic without ever breaking rite or mercy. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Paladins.
